name: Navajo Fry Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a deep fryer to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. In a large bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, and salt.
3. Gradually add warm water to the dry ingredients, stirring until it reaches the consistency of bread dough.
4. Tear off small balls of dough (about 1/4 cup each).
5. Roll out each ball on a lightly dusted surface with cornmeal to about 1/4 inch thickness.
6. Make a hole in the center of each piece.
7. Fry the bread one at a time, turning as soon as it turns golden brown.
8. Drain on absorbent paper and serve hot with honey or powdered sugar. These are also delicious plain or topped with salsa.
